description:
## Overview

Add a `cfg wallpaper` subcommand to the cfg tool that manages wallpaper across different monitor configurations. The wallpaper image should span multiple monitors (by cropping and splitting with ImageMagick) or scale to fit a single monitor, applied via hyprpaper IPC.

## Motivation

The user has two desktop configurations:
- **Work**: Two 1920x1080 monitors side-by-side
- **Home**: One 3440x1440 ultrawide

A single high-resolution wallpaper (e.g., 8443x7501) should work on both — spanning across dual monitors at work, and scaling to fill the ultrawide at home.

## Architecture Decisions

### Config Pattern
`cfg wallpaper` follows the `--get/--set/--apply` pattern like `cfg theme` and `cfg font`. Wallpaper preferences (path, gravity, cache dir) are persisted to `[wallpaper]` section in `cfg/config.toml`.

However, `--apply` does NOT call `update_apps()` (template rendering). Instead it runs a custom pipeline:
1. Detect monitors via `hyprctl monitors -j`
2. Process image with ImageMagick (`magick`)
3. Apply per-monitor wallpapers via hyprpaper IPC (`hyprctl hyprpaper preload/wallpaper`)

### IPC Strategy
- **Phase 1 (this epic)**: One-shot CLI. External scripts call `cfg wallpaper --apply`. A thin event listener script reacts to Hyprland socket2 events (`monitoradded`/`monitorremoved`) to re-apply.
- **Phase 2 (future epic)**: cfg daemon mode with Unix socket. Hot state, event subscriptions, reactive cascading.

### Module Design
Wallpaper processing logic lives in `src/wallpaper.rs` as a standalone module. It takes config + monitor info as input and produces IPC commands as output. This allows reuse from both CLI (now) and daemon (future).

### hyprpaper Integration
hyprpaper runs as a systemd user service. Its static config (`hyprpaper.conf`) is reduced to just `splash = false`. All wallpaper assignments happen at runtime via IPC:
- `hyprctl hyprpaper preload "/path/to/image.jpg"`
- `hyprctl hyprpaper wallpaper "MONITOR,/path/to/image.jpg"`
- `hyprctl hyprpaper unload unused`

### ImageMagick Pipeline
- Single monitor: `magick source.jpg -resize "WxH^" -gravity Center -extent "WxH" -quality 95 out.jpg`
- Multi-monitor: resize+extent to combined canvas, then crop per-monitor slices with `+repage`
- Cache key: `md5(source_mtime:gravity:sorted_monitor_layout)`
- Cache location: `~/.cache/wallpapers/`

## Key Files

### Existing (to modify)
- `tools/cfg/src/main.rs` — Add `Command::Wallpaper` variant
- `tools/cfg/src/config.rs` — Add `WallpaperConfig` struct
- `tools/cfg/src/render.rs` — Add wallpaper vars to `build_context()`
- `tools/cfg/tests/cli.rs` — Add wallpaper flag tests
- `tools/cfg/Cargo.toml` — Any new deps
- `cfg/config.toml` — Add `[wallpaper]` section
- `arch/hyprpaper/hyprpaper.conf` — Reduce to `splash = false`

### New (to create)
- `tools/cfg/src/wallpaper.rs` — Wallpaper processing module
- `arch/wallpaper/set-wallpaper.sh` — Event listener script
- `arch/wallpaper/wallpaper.conf` — Hyprland exec-once drop-in
- `arch/wallpaper/dot.yaml` — Rotz module definition

## References
- hyprpaper IPC: https://wiki.hypr.land/Hypr-Ecosystem/hyprpaper/
- Hyprland socket2 events: https://wiki.hypr.land/IPC/
- swww daemon model: https://github.com/LGFae/swww
- Original issue: .dotfiles-85y
title: cfg wallpaper: Multi-monitor wallpaper management

description:
## Summary

Create a module that queries the current monitor layout from Hyprland via hyprctl and returns structured data for the wallpaper processing pipeline.

## What to Do

### 1. Create `tools/cfg/src/wallpaper/monitors.rs`

(The wallpaper module will be a directory: `src/wallpaper/mod.rs` + submodules)

Define the data types:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Monitor {
    pub name: String,     // e.g. "HDMI-A-1"
    pub width: u32,       // e.g. 1920
    pub height: u32,      // e.g. 1080
    pub x: i32,           // position offset, e.g. 0
    pub y: i32,           // position offset, e.g. 0
    pub scale: f64,       // e.g. 1.0
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct MonitorLayout {
    pub monitors: Vec<Monitor>,  // sorted left-to-right by x position
}
```

### 2. Implement monitor detection

```rust
impl MonitorLayout {
    /// Query hyprctl monitors -j and parse the JSON output
    pub fn detect() -> Result<Self, String> { ... }

    /// Total combined width (sum of all monitor widths)
    pub fn total_width(&self) -> u32 { ... }

    /// Maximum height across all monitors
    pub fn max_height(&self) -> u32 { ... }

    /// Whether this is a single-monitor setup
    pub fn is_single(&self) -> bool { ... }

    /// The leftmost X offset (for normalizing crop positions)
    pub fn min_x(&self) -> i32 { ... }
}
```

Detection runs: `hyprctl monitors -j` and parses the JSON output using `serde_json`.

The JSON from hyprctl looks like:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "HDMI-A-1",
    "width": 1920,
    "height": 1080,
    "x": 0,
    "y": 0,
    "scale": 1.0,
    ...many other fields...
  }
]
```

Only extract the fields we need. Use `serde_json::Value` to avoid depending on the full hyprctl schema (it changes between versions). Sort monitors by x position ascending.

### 3. Handle edge cases

- No monitors detected → return error
- hyprctl not available (not running Hyprland) → return clear error message
- Monitors with different heights → use max height for spanning canvas
- Monitors with scale != 1.0 → use effective resolution (width/scale, height/scale) — but document this as a known limitation for now, use raw dimensions

### 4. Tests

Unit tests with mock JSON data (don't require running Hyprland):
- Parse two side-by-side 1080p monitors
- Parse single 3440x1440 ultrawide
- Parse monitors with non-zero y offset
- Error on empty monitor list
- Error on malformed JSON

Extract the JSON parsing into a `MonitorLayout::from_json(json: &str)` method so it can be tested without calling hyprctl.

## Key Files
- `tools/cfg/src/wallpaper/monitors.rs` — New file
- `tools/cfg/src/wallpaper/mod.rs` — New file (module root)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `MonitorLayout::detect()` calls hyprctl and returns parsed monitors
- `MonitorLayout::from_json()` parses mock data in tests
- Monitors sorted by x position
- Helper methods: total_width, max_height, is_single, min_x
- All edge cases handled with clear error messages
- `cargo test` passes
title: Implement monitor detection module

description:
## Summary

Add the `cfg wallpaper` subcommand to the CLI with proper flag design, following the TDD pattern mandated by tools/cfg/CLAUDE.md.

## IMPORTANT: Read tools/cfg/CLAUDE.md First

The CLAUDE.md for cfg requires:
1. Map out all valid permutations explicitly before implementing
2. Whitelist valid combinations rather than blacklisting invalid ones
3. Write tests for all valid combinations AND invalid combinations
4. Consider subcommands over flags when modes have distinct option sets

## What to Do

### 1. Add Command::Wallpaper to the enum in `tools/cfg/src/main.rs`

```rust
/// Wallpaper configuration
Wallpaper {
    /// Get a specific value (path, gravity, cache_dir)
    #[arg(long, group = "mode")]
    get: Option<String>,
    /// Set a value (format: key=value)
    #[arg(long, group = "mode")]
    set: Option<String>,
    /// After --set, apply wallpaper. Standalone: re-apply current wallpaper.
    #[arg(long)]
    apply: bool,
},
```

### 2. Flag Permutation Matrix

Valid combinations:
| Flags | Behavior |
|---|---|
| (none) | Print current wallpaper config (path, gravity, cache_dir) |
| `--get <key>` | Print single value |
| `--set key=value` | Set value, save config |
| `--set key=value --apply` | Set value, save config, then apply wallpaper |
| `--apply` | Re-apply current wallpaper (no config change) |

Invalid combinations (enforced by clap `group = "mode"`):
| Flags | Why invalid |
|---|---|
| `--get X --set Y` | Can't get and set simultaneously |

Note: `--apply` does NOT conflict with `--set` (it's a modifier on set) AND it can be used standalone (unlike theme/font where --apply requires --set). This is because re-applying the current wallpaper is a valid use case (e.g., after monitor change).

### 3. Add dispatch arm in `main()`

For now, implement only the config get/set/display logic. The `--apply` arm should print "wallpaper apply not yet implemented" and exit 0. The actual apply pipeline is a separate task.

Follow the exact pattern of `Command::Theme` dispatch for get/set. For the "no flags" case, print:
```
path=<value>
gravity=<value>
cache_dir=<value>
```

For `--set`, validate keys:
- `path` — any string
- `gravity` — validate against: Center, North, South, East, West, NorthEast, NorthWest, SouthEast, SouthWest
- `cache_dir` — any string
- Anything else → error with "Unknown key: X (valid: path, gravity, cache_dir)"

Use `config.set(format!("wallpaper.{}", key), value)` to delegate to Config::set().

### 4. Write tests FIRST (TDD)

In `tools/cfg/tests/cli.rs`, add:

```rust
// =============================================================================
// WALLPAPER command tests
// =============================================================================

// Invalid combinations
#[test]
fn wallpaper_get_rejects_set() { ... "cannot be used with" ... }

// Valid combinations (should NOT produce clap conflict errors)
#[test]
fn wallpaper_shows_current() { cfg().arg("wallpaper").assert().success(); }

#[test]
fn wallpaper_get_path() { cfg().args(["wallpaper", "--get", "path"]).assert().success(); }

#[test]
fn wallpaper_apply_standalone_accepted() {
    // --apply alone should be accepted by clap (may fail at runtime, that's ok)
    let result = cfg().args(["wallpaper", "--apply"]).assert();
    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&result.get_output().stderr);
    assert!(!stderr.contains("cannot be used with"));
}

#[test]
fn wallpaper_set_with_apply_accepted() {
    let result = cfg().args(["wallpaper", "--set", "gravity=North", "--apply"]).assert();
    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&result.get_output().stderr);
    assert!(!stderr.contains("cannot be used with"));
}
```

### 5. Rebuild binary

After implementation, run:
```bash
cd tools/cfg && cargo build --release
cp target/release/cfg ../../cfg/bin/cfg
```

The binary is committed to the repo (see tools/cfg/dot.yaml).

## Key Files
- `tools/cfg/src/main.rs` — Command enum + dispatch
- `tools/cfg/tests/cli.rs` — TDD tests
- `tools/cfg/CLAUDE.md` — TDD requirements (READ THIS)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cfg wallpaper` prints current config
- `cfg wallpaper --get path` prints wallpaper path
- `cfg wallpaper --set path=/home/carlos/Pictures/Wallpapers/peach-sunset.jpg` saves to config
- `cfg wallpaper --set gravity=North` saves, `--set gravity=Invalid` errors
- `cfg wallpaper --apply` accepted (placeholder output ok)
- `cfg wallpaper --set path=X --apply` accepted
- `cfg wallpaper --get X --set Y` rejected by clap
- All tests pass: `cargo test`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: "Add [wallpaper] section to Config struct" (needs WallpaperConfig fields)
title: Add cfg wallpaper CLI subcommand skeleton + TDD

description:
## Summary

Add wallpaper configuration fields to the cfg Config struct so wallpaper preferences persist across reboots.

## What to Do

### 1. Add WallpaperConfig struct to `tools/cfg/src/config.rs`

```rust
#[derive(Deserialize, Serialize, Debug, Clone)]
pub struct WallpaperConfig {
    #[serde(default = "default_wallpaper_path")]
    pub path: String,
    #[serde(default = "default_wallpaper_gravity")]
    pub gravity: String,
    #[serde(default = "default_wallpaper_cache_dir")]
    pub cache_dir: String,
}
```

Default values:
- `path`: `""` (empty = no wallpaper configured)
- `gravity`: `"Center"`
- `cache_dir`: `"~/.cache/wallpapers"` (use $HOME expansion at runtime, NOT in the default)

### 2. Add `wallpaper` field to `Config` struct

```rust
pub struct Config {
    // ... existing fields ...
    #[serde(default)]
    pub wallpaper: WallpaperConfig,
}
```

### 3. Add get/set support

Add match arms to `Config::get()` and `Config::set()` for:
- `wallpaper.path` — any string, no validation
- `wallpaper.gravity` — validate against: Center, North, South, East, West, NorthEast, NorthWest, SouthEast, SouthWest
- `wallpaper.cache_dir` — any string, no validation

### 4. Add default implementations

Follow the existing pattern of `default_mono()`, `default_sans()`, etc.

### 5. Update cfg/config.toml

Add section:
```toml
[wallpaper]
path = ""
gravity = "Center"
cache_dir = "~/.cache/wallpapers"
```

### 6. Tests

Add unit tests for:
- `Config::get("wallpaper.path")` returns the value
- `Config::set("wallpaper.gravity", "North")` succeeds
- `Config::set("wallpaper.gravity", "invalid")` fails with error
- Round-trip: load → set → save → load → get returns same value
- Default config has empty wallpaper path

## Key Files
- `tools/cfg/src/config.rs` — Main changes
- `cfg/config.toml` — Add defaults

## Acceptance Criteria
- `WallpaperConfig` struct with path, gravity, cache_dir
- All three fields accessible via `Config::get()` and `Config::set()`
- Gravity validated against ImageMagick gravity constants
- Existing config.toml files without `[wallpaper]` section load without error (serde defaults)
- `cargo test` passes
title: Add [wallpaper] section to Config struct

description: Replace the four special workspace scripts (hyprclaude, hyprclipse, hyprgit, qalculate inline) and shared bash library (hyprspace-lib.sh) with a single config-driven Rust binary. Supports per-workspace behavior via TOML config: context-aware vs global, single vs multi-instance, spawn commands. Three modes per workspace: toggle (context-aware show/hide), spawn (explicit new instance), raw (bare togglespecialworkspace). Dismisses pyprland scratchpads when showing special workspaces. See plan at .claude/plans/imperative-beaming-firefly.md
title: Unified hyprspace manager (Rust)

description:
Create tools/hyprspace/ following tools/cfg/ pattern. Initialize Cargo project with clap (derive), serde, toml, serde_json. Define four subcommands: toggle <workspace>, spawn <workspace>, raw <workspace>, dismiss-scratchpads. All subcommands stub to unimplemented!(). Create dot.yaml with cargo build --release install and binary symlink to ~/.local/bin/hyprspace.

Module files to create (empty placeholders): config.rs, hyprctl.rs, context.rs, workspace.rs, scratchpads.rs, lock.rs, notify.rs

Tests (tests/cli.rs): Use assert_cmd + predicates (same as tools/cfg/tests/cli.rs pattern). Test: toggle/spawn/raw require workspace arg, dismiss-scratchpads takes no args, bare hyprspace fails.

Cargo.toml deps: clap 4 (derive), serde 1 (derive), toml 0.8, serde_json 1. Dev: assert_cmd 2, predicates 3.

dot.yaml: linux[whoami.distro^="Arch"] with installs cmd "cd tools/hyprspace && cargo build --release" and links target/release/hyprspace: ~/.local/bin/hyprspace

Reference files: tools/cfg/Cargo.toml, tools/cfg/src/main.rs, tools/cfg/tests/cli.rs, tools/cfg/dot.yaml

Verification: cargo test passes, cargo build --release succeeds, ./target/release/hyprspace --help shows all four subcommands.
title: S0-T1: Project scaffold and CLI skeleton

description:
Replace all old script/inline keybindings with hyprspace invocations.

ai/claude/claude.conf:
  bind = SUPER, grave, exec, app2unit -- hyprspace toggle ai
  bind = SUPER SHIFT, grave, exec, app2unit -- hyprspace spawn ai
  bind = SUPER CTRL, grave, exec, app2unit -- hyprspace raw ai

arch/clipse/clipse.conf:
  bind = $mainMod, V, exec, hyprspace toggle clipboard
  bind = $mainMod CTRL, V, exec, hyprspace raw clipboard

tools/lazygit/lazygit.conf:
  bind = SUPER, G, exec, hyprspace toggle lazygit
  bind = SUPER CTRL, G, exec, hyprspace raw lazygit

arch/qalculate/qalculate.conf:
  bind = $mainMod, C, exec, hyprspace toggle calculator
  bind = $mainMod CTRL, C, exec, hyprspace raw calculator

Verification: hyprctl reload succeeds. Test each keybind manually.

- arch/clipse/hyprclipse.sh

Before deleting, verify no remaining references:
  grep -r "hyprspace-lib" --include="*.sh" --include="*.yaml" --include="*.yml"
  grep -r "hyprclaude\|hyprgit\|hyprclipse" --include="*.conf" --include="*.yaml" --include="*.yml"
Both should return empty.

Also delete or empty lib/hyprspace/dot.yaml to stop deploying the dead library.

description:
Write tools/hyprspace/README.md and ensure clean build.

README sections: Overview, Installation (via rotz), Config format (TOML schema), CLI reference (4 subcommands), Keybinding pattern (three-tier SUPER/SHIFT/CTRL), Adding a new workspace (step-by-step).

Verification: cargo clippy clean, cargo test green, README renders correctly.

S1-T1: Config module

description:
Implement src/hyprctl.rs — all hyprctl queries and dispatches as typed functions. This is the ONLY module that invokes hyprctl.

Data types (Deserialize):
- ActiveWindow { class, pid: i64, initial_title (serde rename initialTitle) }
- Client { address, class, initial_title, workspace: WorkspaceRef }
- WorkspaceRef { name }
- Monitor { focused: bool, special_workspace: SpecialWorkspace }
- SpecialWorkspace { name }

IPC functions (return Result<T, String>):
- get_active_window() — hyprctl activewindow -j
- get_clients() — hyprctl clients -j
- get_monitors() — hyprctl monitors -j
- dispatch_toggle_special(workspace) — hyprctl dispatch togglespecialworkspace
- dispatch_focus_window(address) — hyprctl dispatch focuswindow address:X

Pure helper functions (unit-testable):
- get_focused_special_workspace(monitors: &[Monitor]) -> Option<&str>
- find_window_by_class_and_title(clients, class, title) -> Option<&Client>
- find_windows_by_class(clients, class) -> Vec<&Client>

Unit tests: focused special workspace returns None when empty, returns name when set. Window finding with match/no-match/multiple.

Reference: lib/hyprspace/hyprspace-lib.sh functions hyprspace_is_workspace_visible, hyprspace_find_window for exact hyprctl commands.

Verification: cargo test hyprctl — pure logic tests pass without Hyprland.
title: S1-T2: Hyprctl IPC module

description:
Implement src/context.rs — detect CWD from active window. Direct port of hyprspace_get_kitty_context() from lib/hyprspace/hyprspace-lib.sh.

Public functions:
- detect_cwd(window: &ActiveWindow) -> Option<PathBuf> — dispatch on window.class:
  - "kitty" -> query_kitty_socket(pid)
  - ends with "_modal" -> parse_modal_title(initial_title)
  - "org.kde.dolphin" -> parse_dolphin_title(initial_title)
  - else -> None
- find_git_root(cwd: &Path) -> Option<PathBuf> — run git -C cwd rev-parse --show-toplevel

Private functions:
- query_kitty_socket(pid) — try unix:@mykitty-{pid} first via "kitty @ --to unix:@mykitty-{pid} ls", fallback to unix:@mykitty. Parse JSON, find focused window cwd. See hyprspace-lib.sh lines 38-55.
- parse_modal_title(title) — split on ": ", take second part, validate is_dir()
- parse_dolphin_title(title) — split on " —", take first part, validate is_dir()

IMPORTANT: Never fall back to $HOME. Return None if no context found.

Unit tests: parse_modal_title valid/invalid/no-separator. parse_dolphin_title with/without dash suffix.

Reference: lib/hyprspace/hyprspace-lib.sh lines 33-73.

Verification: cargo test context
title: S2-T1: Context detection module

description:
Implement src/lock.rs (spawn lockfile) and src/notify.rs (notify-send wrapper).

Lock (src/lock.rs):
Port of hyprspace_wait_for_window() lock logic from hyprspace-lib.sh lines 136-161. SpawnLock struct with try_acquire(context_id: &str) -> Option<Self>. Sanitize context_id (replace / and spaces with _). Lockfile at /tmp/hyprspace-{sanitized}.lock. Use fs2 crate for try_lock_exclusive(). Drop impl removes lockfile. Add fs2 = "0.4" to Cargo.toml.

Notify (src/notify.rs):
Simple wrapper: notify(urgency: Urgency, summary: &str, body: &str). Urgency enum: Low, Normal, Critical. Runs notify-send -u {urgency} hyprspace {body}. Ignore errors.

Unit tests for lock: acquire succeeds, double acquire fails (same context_id), releases on drop, path sanitization.

Reference: hyprspace-lib.sh lines 136-161.

Verification: cargo test lock
title: S2-T2: Lock and notify modules

description:
Implement src/scratchpads.rs and wire dismiss-scratchpads subcommand in main.rs.

scratchpads.rs: pub fn dismiss_all(names: &[String]) — for each name, run pypr-client hide {name}. Ignore errors.

main.rs wiring: Command::DismissScratchpads -> load config, call scratchpads::dismiss_all(&config.scratchpads.names).

No unit tests (requires running pyprland). Add comment: "Integration only — tested manually."

Pyprland scratchpad names: btop, fastfetch (defined in arch/hyprland/pyprland.toml via include conf.d/).

Verification (manual): hyprspace dismiss-scratchpads with btop open -> btop hides. Nothing open -> no-op exit 0.
title: S3-T1: Scratchpads module and dismiss command

description:
Implement src/workspace.rs toggle() function and wire Command::Toggle in main.rs. This is the core logic task.

pub fn toggle(workspace_name, config):
1. Get workspace config or notify error + return
2. If our workspace visible on focused monitor -> dispatch_toggle_special (hide), return
3. If ws.dismiss_scratchpads -> scratchpads::dismiss_all
4. Match context_type: None -> toggle_global(), Cwd -> toggle_with_cwd(), GitRoot -> toggle_with_git_root()

toggle_global(ws): Find existing window by class. Found -> focus_and_show. Not found -> spawn_and_wait.

toggle_with_cwd(ws):
- Get active window, detect_cwd. If CWD found: build title "{prefix}: {cwd}", find matching window.
  - Found -> focus_and_show
  - Not found + multi_instance -> spawn_and_wait with context
  - Not found + single_instance -> show any existing window of that class (never spawn)
- If no CWD: show any existing window (check extra_classes too for Gemini), never spawn

toggle_with_git_root(ws): Same pattern but uses find_git_root. Not a git repo -> notify error.

focus_and_show: dispatch_toggle_special then dispatch_focus_window
spawn_and_wait: Acquire SpawnLock. Interpolate {title} and {context} in spawn_command. Poll hyprctl clients every 100ms for 2s. Focus when found.

Reference files (READ THESE): ai/claude/hyprclaude.sh, tools/lazygit/hyprgit.sh, arch/clipse/hyprclipse.sh, lib/hyprspace/hyprspace-lib.sh

Verification (manual): Build and test toggle for each workspace type.
title: S3-T2: Workspace toggle logic

description:
Wire Command::Spawn and Command::Raw in main.rs, implement workspace::spawn() and workspace::raw().

pub fn spawn(workspace_name, config):
- Get workspace config. If !multi_instance -> notify "single-instance", return
- Dismiss scratchpads if configured
- Get active window, detect context. If no context -> notify "No context for spawn", return
- Call spawn_and_wait (reuse from toggle logic)

pub fn raw(workspace_name, _config):
- Just dispatch_toggle_special(workspace_name). No context, no scratchpad dismissal.

Verification: hyprspace spawn ai (creates new Claude), hyprspace spawn lazygit (rejects: single-instance), hyprspace raw ai (bare toggle).
title: S3-T3: Spawn and raw subcommands

description:
Create tools/hyprspace/config.toml with all four workspace definitions. Update dot.yaml to symlink to ~/.config/hyprspace/config.toml.

Workspaces:
- ai: window_class=claude_modal, title_prefix=claude, context_type=cwd, multi_instance=true, dismiss_scratchpads=true, extra_classes=[chrome-gemini.google.com__-Default], spawn_command=[kitty, --class, claude_modal, --title, {title}, --directory, {context}, --hold, claude]
- clipboard: window_class=clipse_scratch, window_title=clipse, context_type=none, multi_instance=false, spawn_command=[kitty, --class, clipse_scratch, --title, clipse, clipse]
- lazygit: window_class=lazygit_modal, title_prefix=lazygit, context_type=git_root, multi_instance=false, spawn_command=[kitty, --class, lazygit_modal, --title, {title}, --directory, {context}, lazygit]
- calculator: window_class=qalculate-gtk, context_type=none, multi_instance=false, dismiss_scratchpads=false, spawn_via_desktop=true, spawn_command=[app2unit, qalculate-gtk.desktop]
- scratchpads.names = [btop, fastfetch]

dot.yaml update: Add link config.toml: ~/.config/hyprspace/config.toml

Verification: hyprspace toggle ai reads config and works.
title: S4-T1: Write production config.toml

description: Create arch/wallpaper/wallpaper-listener.sh. Listens to Hyprland socket2 for monitoradded/monitorremoved events via socat, calls 'cfg wallpaper --apply' on change. Run as systemd user service or exec-once. Depends on: apply pipeline.
title: Add monitor-change event listener script

description: Create tools/cfg/src/wallpaper/processing.rs. Functions: resize_and_crop(source, width, height, gravity) for single monitor, create_spanning_image(source, total_w, max_h, gravity) + extract_slice(spanning, w, h, x_offset) for multi-monitor. Uses magick CLI. Cache key = md5(mtime:gravity:layout). Output to ~/.cache/wallpapers/. Include +repage after -crop.
title: Implement ImageMagick image processing module

description: Orchestrate the full apply flow in the Command::Wallpaper dispatch arm: load config -> MonitorLayout::detect() -> if single monitor: resize_and_crop -> preload + set. If multi: create_spanning -> extract_slice per monitor -> preload + set each -> unload_unused. Depends on: config struct, CLI skeleton, monitor detection, image processing, hyprpaper IPC.
title: Wire cfg wallpaper --apply pipeline

description: Split ~/.config/zsh/*.zsh into env.d/ (PATH/env exports sourced from .zshenv) and interactive-only files (plugins, aliases, evals sourced from .zshrc). Currently all config is sourced from .zshrc only, which means non-interactive shells (e.g. kitty --hold <cmd>) lack PATH entries. The hyprclaude fix (zsh -ic) works around this, but the proper fix is separating env from interactive config.
title: Refactor zsh conf.d: split env vs interactive config
